  with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\")\n\n    def test_merge_groupby_multiple_column_with_categorical_column(self):\n        # GH 16454\n        df = pd.DataFrame({\"x\": [0], \"y\": [0], \"z\": pd.Categorical([0])})\n        result = merge_asof(df, df, on=\"x\", by=[\"y\", \"z\"])\n        expected = pd.DataFrame({\"x\": [0], \"y\": [0], \"z\": pd.Categorical([0])})\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\n        \"func\", [lambda x: x, lambda x: to_datetime(x)], ids=[\"numeric\", \"datetime\"]\n    )\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"side\", [\"left\", \"right\"])\n    def test_merge_on_nans(self, func, side):\n        # GH 23189\n        msg = f\"Merge keys contain null values on {side} side\"\n        nulls = func([1.0, 5.0, np.nan])\n        non_nulls = func([1.0, 5.0, 10.0])\n        df_null = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": nulls, \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n        df = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": non_nulls, \"right_val\": [1, 6, 11]})\n\n        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=msg):\n            if side == \"left\":\n                merge_asof(df_null, df, on=\"a\")\n            else:\n                merge_asof(df, df_null, on=\"a\")\n\n    def test_by_nullable(self, any_numeric_ea_dtype, using_infer_string):\n        # Note: this test passes if instead of using pd.array we use\n        #  np.array([np.nan, 1]).  Other than that, I (@jbrockmendel)\n        #  have NO IDEA what the expected behavior is.\n        # TODO(GH#32306): may be relevant to the expected behavior here.\n\n        arr = pd.array([pd.NA, 0, 1], dtype=any_numeric_ea_dtype)\n        if arr.dtype.kind in [\"i\", \"u\"]:\n            max_val = np.iinfo(arr.dtype.numpy_dtype).max\n        else:\n            max_val = np.finfo(arr.dtype.numpy_dtype).max\n        # set value s.t. (at least for integer dtypes) arr._values_for_argsort\n        #  is not an injection\n        arr[2] = max_val\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"by_col1\": arr,\n                \"by_col2\": [\"HELLO\", \"To\", \"You\"],\n                \"on_col\": [2, 4, 6],\n                \"value\": [\"a\", \"c\", \"e\"],\n            }\n        )\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"by_col1\": arr,\n                \"by_col2\": [\"WORLD\", \"Wide\", \"Web\"],\n                \"on_col\": [1, 2, 6],\n                \"value\": [\"b\", \"d\", \"f\"],\n            }\n        )\n\n        result = merge_asof(left, right, by=[\"by_col1\", \"by_col2\"], on=\"on_col\")\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"by_col1\": arr,\n                \"by_col2\": [\"HELLO\", \"To\", \"You\"],\n                \"on_col\": [2, 4, 6],\n                \"value_x\": [\"a\", \"c\", \"e\"],\n            }\n        )\n        expected[\"value_y\"] = np.array([np.nan, np.nan, np.nan], dtype=object)\n        if using_infer_string:\n            expected[\"value_y\"] = expected[\"value_y\"].astype(\"string[pyarrow_numpy]\")\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_merge_by_col_tz_aware(self):\n        # GH 21184\n        left = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"by_col\": pd.DatetimeIndex([\"2018-01-01\"]).tz_localize(\"UTC\"),\n                \"on_col\": [2],\n                \"values\": [\"a\"],\n            }\n        )\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"by_col\": pd.DatetimeIndex([\"2018-01-01\"]).tz_localize(\"UTC\"),\n                \"on_col\": [1],\n                \"values\": [\"b\"],\n            }\n        )\n        result = merge_asof(left, right, by=\"by_col\", on=\"on_col\")\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            [[pd.Timestamp(\"2018-01-01\", tz=\"UTC\"), 2, \"a\", \"b\"]],\n            columns=[\"by_col\", \"on_col\", \"values_x\", \"values_y\"],\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_by_mixed_tz_aware(self, using_infer_string):\n        # GH 27642\n        if unit == \"s\":\n            pytest.skip(\n                \"This test is invalid with unit='s' because that would \"\n                \"round left['time']\"\n            )\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame(\n            list(zip([0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25], [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5])),\n            columns=[\"time\", \"left\"],\n        )\n\n        left[\"time\"] = pd.to_timedelta(left[\"time\"], \"ms\").astype(f\"m8[{unit}]\")\n\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            list(zip([0, 3, 9, 12, 15, 18], [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5])),\n            columns=[\"time\", \"right\"],\n        )\n\n        right[\"time\"] = pd.to_timedelta(right[\"time\"], \"ms\").astype(f\"m8[{unit}]\")\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            list(\n                zip(\n                    [0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25],\n                    [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5],\n                    [0, np.nan, 2, 4, np.nan, np.nan],\n                )\n            ),\n            columns=[\"time\", \"left\", \"right\"],\n        )\n\n        expected[\"time\"] = pd.to_timedelta(expected[\"time\"], \"ms\").astype(f\"m8[{unit}]\")\n\n        result = merge_asof(\n            left, right, on=\"time\", tolerance=Timedelta(\"1ms\"), direction=\"nearest\"\n        )\n\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_int_type_tolerance(self, any_int_dtype):\n        #    def test_multi_index_left(self, trades, quotes):\n        # MultiIndex is prohibited\n        trades = trades.set_index([\"time\", \"price\"])\n        quotes = quotes.set_index(\"time\")\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=\"left can only have one index\"):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, left_index=True, right_index=True)\n\n    def test_multi_index_right(self, trades, together is prohibited\n        trades = trades.set_index(\"time\")\n        quotes = quotes.set_index(\"time\")\n        msg = 'Can only pass argument \"left_on\" OR \"left_index\" not both.'\n           [\n                [to_datetime(\"20160602\"), 1, \"a\"],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160602\"), 2, \"a\"],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160603\"), 1, \"b\"],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160603\"), 2, \"b\"],\n            ],\n            columns=[\"time\", \"k1\", \"k2\"],\n        ).set_index(\"time\")\n        # different dtype for the index\n        left.index = left.index - pd.Timestamp(0)\n\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            [\n                [to_datetime(\"20160502\"), 1, \"a\", 1.0],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160502\"), 2, \"a\", 2.0],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160503\"), 1, \"b\", 3.0],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160503\"), 2, \"b\", 4.0],\n            ],\n            columns=[\"time\", \"k1\", \"k2\", \"value\"],\n        ).set_index(\"time\")\n\n        msg = \"incompatible merge keys\"\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(left, right, left_index=True, right_index=True, by=[\"k1\", \"k2\"])\n\n    def test_multiby_indexed(self):\n        # GH15676\n        left = pd.DataFrame(\n            [\n                [to_datetime(\"20160602\"), 1, \"a\"],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160602\"), 2, \"a\"],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160603\"), 1, \"b\"],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160603\"), 2, \"b\"],\n            ],\n            columns=[\"time\", \"k1\", \"k2\"],\n        ).set_index(\"time\")\n\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            [\n                [to_datetime(\"20160502\"), 1, \"a\", 1.0],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160502\"), 2, \"a\", 2.0],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160503\"), 1, \"b\", 3.0],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160503\"), 2, \"b\", 4.0],\n            ],\n            columns=[\"time\", \"k1\", \"k2\", \"value\"],\n        ).set_index(\"time\")\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            [\n                [to_datetime(\"20160602\"), 1, \"a\", 1.0],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160602\"), 2, \"a\", 2.0],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160603\"), 1, \"b\", 3.0],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160603\"), 2, \"b\", 4.0],\n            ],\n            columns=[\"time\", \"k1\", \"k2\", \"value\"],\n        ).set_index(\"time\")\n\n        result = merge_asof(\n            left, right, left_index=True, right_index=True, by=[\"k1\", \"k2\"]\n        )\n\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(expected, result)\n\n        with pytest.raises(\n            MergeError, match=\"left_by and right_by must be the same length\"\n        ):\n            merge_asof(\n                left,\n                right,\n                left_index=True,\n                right_index=True,\n                left_by=[\"k1\", \"k2\"],\n                right_by=[\"k1\"],\n            )\n\n    def test_basic2(self, datapath):\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            [\n                [\n                    \"20160525 13:30:00.023\",\n           .reset_index(drop=True)\n        )\n\n        # just use a single ticker\n        expected = f(asof)\n        trades = f(trades)\n        quotes = f(quotes)\n\n        result = merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\")\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_valid_join_keys(self, trades, quotes):\n        msg = r\"incompatible merge keys \\\\[1\\\\] .* must be the same type\"\n\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, left_on=\"time\", right_on=\"bid\", by=\"ticker\")\n\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=\"can only asof on a key for left\"):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=[\"time\", \"ticker\"], by=\"ticker\")\n\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=\"can only asof on a key for left\"):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, by=\"ticker\")\n\n    def test_with_duplicates(self, datapath, trades, quotes, asof):\n        q = (\n            pd.concat([quotes, quotes])\n            .sort_values([\"time\", \"ticker\"])\n            .reset_index(drop=True)\n        )\n        result = merge_asof(trades, q, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\")\n        expected = self.prep_data(asof)\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_with_duplicates_no_on(self):\n        df1 = pd.DataFrame({\"key\": [1, 1, 3], \"left_val\": [1, 2, 3]})\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame({\"key\": [1, 2, 2], \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3]})\n        result = merge_asof(df1, df2, on=\"key\")\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"key\": [1, 1, 3], \"left_val\": [1, 2, 3], \"right_val\": [1, 1, 3]}\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_valid_allow_exact_matches(self, trades, quotes):\n        msg = \"allow_exact_matches must be boolean, passed foo\"\n\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(\n                trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\", allow_exact_matches=\"foo\"\n            )\n\n    def test_valid_tolerance(self, trades, quotes):\n        # dti\n        merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\", tolerance=Timedelta(\"1s\"))\n\n        # integer\n        merge_asof(\n            trades.reset_index(),\n            quotes.reset_index(),\n            on=\"index\",\n            by=\"ticker\",\n            tolerance=1,\n        )\n\n        msg = r\"incompatible tolerance .*, must be compat with type .*\"\n\n        # incompat\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\", tolerance=1)\n\n        # invalid\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(\n                trades.reset_index(),\n                quotes.reset_index(),\n                on=\"index\",\n                by=\"ticker\",\n                tolerance=1.0,\n            )\n\n        msg = \"tolerance must be positive\"\n\n        # invalid negative\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(\n                trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\", tolerance=-Timedelta(\"1s\")\n            )\n\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(\n                trades.reset_index(),\n                quotes.reset_index(),\n                on=\"index\",\n                by=\"ticker\",\n                tolerance=-1,\n            )\n\n    def test_non_sorted(self, trades, quotes):\n        trades = trades.sort_values(\"time\", ascending=False)\n        quotes = quotes.sort_values(\"time\", ascending=False)\n\n        # we require that we are already sorted on time & quotes\n        assert not trades.time.is_monotonic_increasing\n        assert not quotes.time.is_monotonic_increasing\n        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=\"left keys must be sorted\"):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\")\n\n        trades = trades.sort_values(\"time\")\n        assert trades.time.is_monotonic_increasing\n        assert not quotes.time.is_monotonic_increasing\n        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=\"right keys must be sorted\"):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\")\n\n        quotes = quotes.sort_values(\"time\")\n        assert trades.time.is_monotonic_increasing\n        assert quotes.time.is_monotonic_increasing\n\n        # ok, though has dupes\n        merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\")\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\n        \"tolerance_ts\",\n        [Timedelta(\"1day\"), datetime.timedelta(days=1)],\n        ids=[\"Timedelta\", \"datetime.timedelta\"],\n    )\n    def test_tolerance(self, tolerance_ts, trades, quotes, tolerance):\n        result = merge_asof(\n            trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\", tolerance=tolerance_ts\n        )\n        expected = tolerance\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_tolerance_forward(self):\n        # GH14887\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n        right = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 2, 3, 7, 11], \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3, 7, 11]})\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"], \"right_val\": [1, np.nan, 11]}\n        )\n\n        result = merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", direction=\"forward\", tolerance=1)\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_tolerance_nearest(self):\n        # GH14887\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n        right = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 2, 3, 7, 11], \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3, 7, 11]})\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"], \"right_val\": [1, np.nan, 11]}\n        )\n\n        result = merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", direction=\"nearest\", tolerance=1)\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_tolerance_tz(self, unit):\n        # GH 14844\n        left = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"date\": pd.date_range(\n                    start=to_datetime(\"2016-01-02\"),\n                    freq=\"D\",\n                    periods=5,\n                    tz=pytz.timezone(\"UTC\"),\n                    unit=unit,\n                ),\n                \"value1\": np.arange(5),\n            }\n        )\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"date\": pd.date_range(\n                    start=to_datetime(\"2016-01-01\"),\n                    freq=\"D\",\n                    periods=5,\n                    tz=pytz.timezone(\"UTC\"),\n                    unit=unit,\n                ),\n                \"value2\": list(\"ABCDE\"),\n            }\n        )\n        result = merge_asof(left, right, on=\"date\", tolerance=Timedelta(\"1 day\"))\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"date\": pd.date_range(\n                    start=to_datetime(\"2016-01-02\"),\n                    freq=\"D\",\n                    periods=5,\n                    tz=pytz.timezone(\"UTC\"),\n                    unit=unit,\n                ),\n                \"value1\": np.arange(5),\n                \"value2\": list(\"BCDEE\"),\n            }\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_tolerance_float(self):\n        # GH22981\n        left = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1.1, 3.5, 10.9], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"a\": [1.0, merge_asof(df1, df2, on=\"time\")\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"time\": to_datetime([\"2016-07-15 13:30:00.030\"]),\n                \"username\": [\"bob\"],\n                \"version\": [2],\n            }\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n        result = merge_asof(df1, df2, on=\"time\", allow_exact_matches=False)\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"time\": to_datetime([\"2016-07-15 13:30:00.030\"]),\n                \"username\": [\"bob\"],\n                \"version\": [1],\n            }\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n        result = merge_asof(\n            df1,\n            df2,\n            on=\"time\",\n            allow_exact_matches=False,\n tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_by_int(self):\n        # we specialize by type, so test that this is correct\n        df1 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"time\": to_datetime(\n                    [\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.020\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.030\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.040\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.050\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.060\",\n                    ]\n                ),\n                \"key\": [1, 2, 1, 3, 2],\n                \"value1\": [1.1, 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, 1.5],\n            },\n            columns=[\"time\", \"key\", \"value1\"],\n        )\n\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"time\": to_datetime(\n                    [\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.015\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.020\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.025\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.035\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.040\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.055\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.060\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.065\",\n                    ]\n                ),\n                \"key\": [2, 1, 1, 3, 2, 1, 2, 3],\n                \"value2\": [2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.4, 2.5, 2.6, 2.7, 2.8],\n            },\n            columns=[\"time\", \"key\", \"value2\"],\n        )\n\n        result = merge_asof(df1, df2, on=\"time\", by=\"key\")\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"time\": to_datetime(\n                    [\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.020\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.030\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.040\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.050\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.060\",\n                    ]\n                ),\n                \"key\": [1, 2, 1, 3, 2],\n                \"value1\": [1.1, 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, 1.5],\n                \"value2\": [2.2, 2.1, 2.3, 2.4, 2.7],\n            },\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"price\"],\n        )\n\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"price\": [0.0, 1.0, 100.0], \"mpv\": [0.0001, 0.01, 0.05]},\n using_infer_string:\n            msg = \"incompatible merge keys\"\n        else:\n            msg = r\"Incompatible merge dtype, .*, both sides must have numeric dtype\"\n\n        left = match=msg):\n            merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\")\n\n    def test_merge_datatype_categorical_error_raises(self):\n        msg = (\n            r\"incompatible merge keys \\\\[0\\\\] .* both sides category, \"\n            \"but not equal ones\"\n        )\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"left_val\": [1, 5, 10], \"a\": pd.Categorical([\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"])}\n        )\n   with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\")\n\n    def test_merge_groupby_multiple_column_with_categorical_column(self):\n        # GH 16454\n        df = pd.DataFrame({\"x\": [0], \"y\": [0], \"z\": pd.Categorical([0])})\n        result = merge_asof(df, df, on=\"x\", by=[\"y\", \"z\"])\n        expected = pd.DataFrame({\"x\": [0], \"y\": [0], \"z\": pd.Categorical([0])})\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\n        \"func\", [lambda x: x, lambda x: to_datetime(x)], ids=[\"numeric\", \"datetime\"]\n    )\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"side\", [\"left\", \"right\"])\n    def test_merge_on_nans(self, func, side):\n        # GH 23189\n        msg = f\"Merge keys contain null values on {side} side\"\n        nulls = func([1.0, 5.0, np.nan])\n        non_nulls = func([1.0, 5.0, 10.0])\n        df_null = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": nulls, \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n        df = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": non_nulls, \"right_val\": [1, 6, 11]})\n\n        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=msg):\n            if side == \"left\":\n                merge_asof(df_null, df, on=\"a\")\n            else:\n                merge_asof(df, df_null, on=\"a\")\n\n    def test_by_nullable(self, any_numeric_ea_dtype, using_infer_string):\n        # Note: this test passes if instead of using pd.array we use\n        #  np.array([np.nan, 1]).  Other than that, I (@jbrockmendel)\n        #  have NO IDEA what the expected behavior is.\n        # TODO(GH#32306): may be relevant to the expected behavior here.\n\n        arr = pd.array([pd.NA, 0, 1], dtype=any_numeric_ea_dtype)\n        if arr.dtype.kind in [\"i\", \"u\"]:\n            max_val = np.iinfo(arr.dtype.numpy_dtype).max\n        else:\n            max_val = np.finfo(arr.dtype.numpy_dtype).max\n        # set value s.t. (at least for integer dtypes) arr._values_for_argsort\n        #  is not an injection\n        arr[2] = max_val\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"by_col1\": arr,\n                \"by_col2\": [\"HELLO\", \"To\", \"You\"],\n                \"on_col\": [2, 4, 6],\n                \"value\": [\"a\", \"c\", \"e\"],\n            }\n        )\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"by_col1\": arr,\n                \"by_col2\": [\"WORLD\", \"Wide\", \"Web\"],\n                \"on_col\": [1, 2, 6],\n                \"value\": [\"b\", \"d\", \"f\"],\n            }\n        )\n\n        result = merge_asof(left, right, by=[\"by_col1\", \"by_col2\"], on=\"on_col\")\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"by_col1\": arr,\n                \"by_col2\": [\"HELLO\", \"To\", \"You\"],\n                \"on_col\": [2, 4, 6],\n                \"value_x\": [\"a\", \"c\", \"e\"],\n            }\n        )\n        expected[\"value_y\"] = np.array([np.nan, np.nan, np.nan], dtype=object)\n        if using_infer_string:\n            expected[\"value_y\"] = expected[\"value_y\"].astype(\"string[pyarrow_numpy]\")\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_merge_by_col_tz_aware(self):\n        # GH 27642\n        if unit == \"s\":\n            pytest.skip(\n                \"This test is invalid with unit='s' because that would \"\n                \"round left['time']\"\n            )\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame(\n            list(zip([0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25], [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5])),\n            columns=[\"time\", \"left\"],\n        )\n\n        left[\"time\"] = pd.to_timedelta(left[\"time\"], \"ms\").astype(f\"m8[{unit}]\")\n\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            list(zip([0, 3, 9, 12, 15, 18], [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5])),\n            columns=[\"time\", \"right\"],\n        )\n\n        right[\"time\"] = pd.to_timedelta(right[\"time\"], \"ms\").astype(f\"m8[{unit}]\")\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            list(\n                zip(\n                    [0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25],\n                    [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5],\n                    [0, np.nan, 2, 4, np.nan, np.nan],\n                )\n            ),\n            columns=[\"time\", \"left\", \"right\"],\n        )\n\n        expected[\"time\"] = pd.to_timedelta(expected[\"time\"], \"ms\").astype(f\"m8[{unit}]\")\n\n        result = merge_asof(\n            left, right, on=\"time\", tolerance=Timedelta(\"1ms\"), direction=\"nearest\"\n        )\n\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_int_type_tolerance(self, any_int_dtype):\n        # GH 53513\n    left = pd.Series([2], index=[2], name=\"left\")\n    right = pd.Series([1], index=[1], name=\"right\")\n    # set to read-only\n    left.index.values.flags.writeable = False\n    right.index.values.flags.writeable = False\n    result = merge_asof(left, right, left_index=True, right_index=True)\n    expected = pd.DataFrame({\"left\": [2], \"right\": [1]}, index=[2])\n    tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n\ndef test_merge_asof_multiby_with_categorical():\n    # {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01MjLwcH4T9n1t8YodxFWzsD","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01HG2zUCPrx5gdFRWsb9QbHs","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/CHANGES_SUMMARY.md","content":"# Changes Summary: Add indicator Parameter to merge_asof()\n\n## Overview\nAdded support for an `indicator` parameter to `pandas.merge_asof()` that creates a column indicating the merge result, similar to how `pd.merge()` supports `indicator=True`.\n\n## Files Modified\n\n### 1. `/workspace/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py`\n\n#### Changes to `merge_asof()` function:\n- **Line 455**: Added `indicator: str | bool = False` parameter to function signature\n- **Lines 514-520**: Added documentation for the indicator parameter in the docstring:\n  - Explains that `indicator=True` creates a '_merge' column\n  - Explains that a custom column name can be provided as a string\n  - Notes the possible values: 'left_only', 'right_only', or 'both'\n  - Notes that 'both' will never occur in asof merge (it's a left join)\n- **Line 710**: Pass `indicator=indicator` to `_AsOfMerge` constructor\n\n#### Changes to `_OrderedMerge` class:\n- **Line 1919**: Added `indicator: str | bool = False` parameter to `__init__()` signature\n- **Line 1933**: Pass `indicator=indicator` to parent class `_MergeOperation.__init__()`\n\n#### Changes to `_AsOfMerge` class:\n- **Line 1992**: Added `indicator: str | bool = False` parameter to `__init__()` signature\n- **Line 2025**: Pass `indicator=indicator` to parent class `_OrderedMerge.__init__()`\n- **Lines 2314-2335**: Added `get_result()` method override that:\n  - Calls `_indicator_pre_merge()` if indicator is enabled to add marker columns\n  - Performs the asof merge operation via `_get_join_info()` and `_reindex_and_concat()`\n  - Calls `_indicator_post_merge()` if indicator is enabled to create the final indicator column\n  - Follows the same pattern as `_MergeOperation.get_result()` for consistency\n\n## How It Works\n\nThe indicator implementation follows the same pattern as the regular `merge()` function:\n\n1. **Pre-merge preparation**: If `indicator` is True or a string, temporary marker columns (`_left_indicator` and `_right_indicator`) are added to the dataframes with values 1 (left only) and 2 (right only).\n\n2. **Merge operation**: The asof merge is performed as usual.\n\n3. **Post-merge processing**: The marker columns are combined:\n   - Unfilled values (NaN) map to 0\n   - Left marker = 1, right marker = 2\n   - Sum of markers: 1 = 'left_only', 2 = 'right_only', 3 = 'both'\n   - A Categorical column with the computed values is created\n\n4. **Column naming**:\n   - If `indicator=True`, the column is named '_merge'\n   - If `indicator='custom_name'`, the column is named 'custom_name'\n   - Temporary marker columns are removed from the result\n\n## Key Design Decisions\n\n1. **Using parent class methods**: The indicator handling uses inherited methods (`_indicator_pre_merge()`, `_indicator_post_merge()`) from `_MergeOperation` to maintain consistency with the regular merge.\n\n2. **Overriding get_result() in _AsOfMerge**: Since `_OrderedMerge.get_result()` doesn't support indicator and `_AsOfMerge` has different join mechanics, a specialized `get_result()` was added to `_AsOfMerge` that handles the indicator correctly.\n\n3. **Passing indicator through the class hierarchy**: The indicator parameter flows through:\n   - `merge_asof()` → `_AsOfMerge` → `_OrderedMerge` → `_MergeOperation`\n   - Each class properly stores and passes the parameter to its parent\n\n## Test Coverage\n\n### `/workspace/pandas/tests/reshape/merge/test_merge_asof.py`\n\nAdded four new test functions:\n\n1. **`test_merge_asof_indicator()`**:\n   - Tests that `indicator=True` creates a '_merge' column\n   - Verifies the merge indicator values are correct (should all be 'both' in this case since asof is a left join)\n\n2. **`test_merge_asof_indicator_custom_name()`**:\n   - Tests that a custom indicator name is properly created\n   - Verifies '_merge' column is not created when using a custom name\n\n3. **`test_merge_asof_indicator_false()`**:\n   - Tests that `indicator=False` does not create an indicator column\n   - Ensures backward compatibility\n\n4. **`test_merge_asof_indicator_validation()`**:\n   - Tests that invalid indicator values (e.g., integers) raise appropriate errors\n   - Uses the same validation logic as regular merge\n\n## Backward Compatibility\n\n- The `indicator` parameter defaults to `False`, maintaining backward compatibility\n- Existing code without the parameter will work exactly as before\n- The feature is opt-in via the parameter\n\n## Expected Behavior\n\n```python\nimport pandas as pd\n\nleft = pd.DataFrame({'time': [1, 5, 10], 'left_val': ['a', 'b', 'c']})\nright = pd.DataFrame({'time': [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], 'right_val': [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})\n\n# With indicator=True (creates '_merge' column)\nresult = pd.merge_asof(left, right, on='time', indicator=True)\n\n# With custom indicator name\nresult = pd.merge_asof(left, right, on='time', indicator='source')\n```\n\nThe resulting DataFrame will have an additional column indicating which rows came from where.\n\n## Notes\n\n- In asof merge, all rows from the left DataFrame are retained (it's a left join by nature), so the indicator will typically show \"left_only\" only when a left row has no matching right row in the join keys.\n- The indicator creates a Categorical column with three categories: 'left_only', 'right_only', 'both' (though 'both' will not appear when all left rows are included).\n- The implementation reuses the validation and processing logic from `_MergeOperation` to maintain consistency across merge functions.\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":213,"cache_read_input_tokens":89920,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":213},"output_tokens":4,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3d2b2c80-baed-420c-9079-60777dab028e","uuid":"cb891c2a-b24d-4c92-8bbc-f50c569897e5"}

NEW PARAMETER\n) -> DataFrame:\n```\n\n**Changes:**\n- Added `indicator: str | bool = False` parameter\n- Updated docstring to explain the parameter (lines 514-522)\n- Pass indicator to `_AsOfMerge` constructor (line 717)\n\n#### _OrderedMerge Class (Line 1905)\n**Modified `__init__` method:**\n- Added `indicator: str | bool = False` parameter\n- Pass it to parent class `_MergeOperation.__init__()`\n\n#### _AsOfMerge Class (Line 1972)\n**Modified `__init__` method:**\n- Added `indicator: str | bool = False` parameter\n- Pass it to parent class `_OrderedMerge.__init__()` (line 2025)\n\n**Added `get_result()` method (lines 2314-2332):**\n```python\ndef get_result(self, copy: bool | None = True) -> DataFrame:\n    if self.indicator:\n        self.left, self.right = self._indicator_pre_merge(self.left, self.right)\n\n    join_index, left_indexer, right_indexer = self._get_join_info()\n\n    result = self._reindex_and_concat(\n        join_index, left_indexer, right_indexer, copy=copy\n    )\n    result = result.__finalize__(self, method=self._merge_type)\n\n    if self.indicator:\n        result = self._indicator_post_merge(result)\n\n    self._maybe_add_join_keys(result, left_indexer, right_indexer)\n\n    self._maybe_restore_index_levels(result)\n\n    return result.__finalize__(self, method=\"merge\")\n```\n\n### 2. `/workspace/pandas/tests/reshape/merge/test_merge_asof.py`\n\nAdded four test functions (lines 3660-3740):\n\n1. **test_merge_asof_indicator()** - Tests indicator=True\n2. **test_merge_asof_indicator_custom_name()** - Tests custom indicator column name\n3. **test_merge_asof_indicator_false()** - Tests backward compatibility (indicator=False)\n4. **test_merge_asof_indicator_validation()** - Tests validation of invalid values\n\n## How the Implementation Works\n\n### Parameter Flow\n1. User calls `merge_asof(..., indicator=True)` or `merge_asof(..., indicator='custom_name')`\n2. `merge_asof()` passes indicator to `_AsOfMerge` constructor\n3. `_AsOfMerge.__init__()` passes indicator to `_OrderedMerge.__init__()`\n4. `_OrderedMerge.__init__()` passes indicator to `_MergeOperation.__init__()`\n5. `_MergeOperation` stores indicator and provides methods for pre/post merge processing\n\n### Indicator Processing\nThe indicator works by:\n1. **Pre-merge**: Adding temporary marker columns to left and right dataframes\n   - Left: `_left_indicator` = 1\n   - Right: `_right_indicator` = 2\n\n2. **Merge**: Performing the asof join operation\n\n3. **Post-merge**: Combining markers into the final indicator column\n   - After merge, unfilled markers become 0\n   - Combinations: 1 = 'left_only', 2 = 'right_only', 3 = 'both'\n   - Result is a Categorical column with these categories\n\n4. **Cleanup**: Removing temporary marker columns and adding final indicator column\n\n### Column Naming\n- If `indicator=True`: Column is named '_merge' (default)\n- If `indicator='custom_name'`: Column is named 'custom_name'\n- If `indicator=False`: No indicator column (default behavior)\n\n## Usage Examples\n\n### Example 1: Basic indicator usage\n```python\nimport pandas as pd\n\nleft = pd.DataFrame({\n    'time': [1, 5, 10],\n    'value': [10, 50, 100]\n})\n\nright = pd.DataFrame({\n    'time': [1, 2, 3, 6, 7],\n    'price': [100, 110, 120, 160, 170]\n})\n\nresult = pd.merge_asof(left, right, on='time', indicator=True)\n# Result has a '_merge' column showing 'both', 'both', 'both'\n```\n\n### Example 2: Custom indicator name\n```python\nresult = pd.merge_asof(left, right, on='time', indicator='source')\n# Result has a 'source' column instead of '_merge'\n```\n\n### Example 3: With other parameters\n```python\nresult = pd.merge_asof(\n    left, right,\n    on='time',\n    by='ticker',\n    tolerance=pd.Timedelta('1s'),\n    indicator=True\n)\n# Indicator works with all other merge_asof parameters\n```\n\n## Testing\n\nThe implementation includes comprehensive test coverage:\n\n1. **test_merge_asof_indicator()**: Verifies indicator column creation and content\n2. **test_merge_asof_indicator_custom_name()**: Tests custom naming feature\n3. **test_merge_asof_indicator_false()**: Ensures backward compatibility\n4. **test_merge_asof_indicator_validation()**: Tests error handling for invalid values\n\nRun tests with:\n```bash\npytest pandas/tests/reshape/merge/test_merge_asof.py::test_merge_asof_indicator -v\npytest pandas/tests/reshape/merge/test_merge_asof.py::test_merge_asof_indicator_custom_name -v\npytest pandas/tests/reshape/merge/test_merge_asof.py::test_merge_asof_indicator_false -v\npytest pandas/tests/reshape/merge/test_merge_asof.py::test_merge_asof_indicator_validation -v\n```\n\n## Backward Compatibility\n\n✅ The implementation is fully backward compatible:\n- `indicator` parameter defaults to `False`\n- Existing code without the parameter works unchanged\n- No breaking changes to function signatures\n- No changes to default behavior\n\n## Error Handling\n\nThe implementation validates the indicator parameter:\n- Only accepts boolean or string values\n- Raises `ValueError` with message: \"indicator option can only accept boolean or string arguments\" for invalid values\n- Checks for conflicting column names (_left_indicator, _right_indicator, or custom indicator name)\n\n## Design Notes\n\n### Why Override get_result() in _AsOfMerge?\n- `_OrderedMerge.get_result()` doesn't support indicator\n- `_AsOfMerge` has specific join mechanics requiring custom handling\n- Overriding allows proper integration of pre-merge and post-merge indicator processing\n- Maintains consistency with `_MergeOperation.get_result()`'s indicator handling\n\n### Reusing Indicator Methods\n- `_indicator_pre_merge()` - Adds temporary marker columns\n- `_indicator_post_merge()` - Combines markers into final indicator\n- These inherited methods ensure consistency with regular merge functionality\n- Validation logic is the same across all merge types\n\n## Implementation Checklist\n\n✅ Added `indicator` parameter to `merge_asof()` function signature\n✅ Updated docstring with indicator documentation\n✅ Modified `_OrderedMerge.__init__()` to accept and pass indicator\n✅ Modified `_AsOfMerge.__init__()` to accept and pass indicator\n✅ Implemented `_AsOfMerge.get_result()` with indicator support\n✅ Added comprehensive test cases\n✅ Verified backward compatibility\n✅ Ensured error handling consistency with `pd.merge()`\n✅ Compiled and syntax-checked all modified files\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"cache_creation_input_tokens":436,"cache_read_input_tokens":95389,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":436},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3d2b2c80-baed-420c-9079-60777dab028e","uuid":"eef6c503-fd76-42e3-866f-8ec43a0fef98"}

`_right_indicator` = 2\n\n2. **Merge**: Performing the asof join operation\n\n3. **Post-merge**: Combining markers into the final indicator column\n   - After merge, unfilled markers become 0\n   - Combinations: 1 = 'left_only', 2 = 'right_only', 3 = 'both'\n   - Result is a Categorical column with these categories\n\n4. **Cleanup**: Removing temporary marker columns and adding final indicator column\n\n### Column Naming\n- If `indicator=True`: Column is named '_merge' (default)\n- If `indicator='custom_name'`: Column is named 'custom_name'\n- If `indicator=False`: No indicator column (default behavior)\n\n## Usage Examples\n\n### Example 1: Basic indicator usage\n```python\nimport pandas as pd\n\nleft = pd.DataFrame({\n    'time': [1, 5, 10],\n    'value': [10, 50, 100]\n})\n\nright = pd.DataFrame({\n    'time': [1, 2, 3, 6, 7],\n    'price': [100, 110, 120, 160, 170]\n})\n\nresult = pd.merge_asof(left, right, on='time', indicator=True)\n# Result has a '_merge' column showing 'both', 'both', 'both'\n```\n\n### Example 2: Custom indicator name\n```python\nresult = pd.merge_asof(left, right, on='time', indicator='source')\n# Result has a 'source' column instead of '_merge'\n```\n\n### Example 3: With other parameters\n```python\nresult = pd.merge_asof(\n    left, right,\n    on='time',\n    by='ticker',\n    tolerance=pd.Timedelta('1s'),\n    indicator=True\n)\n# Indicator works with all other merge_asof parameters\n```\n\n## Testing\n\nThe implementation includes comprehensive test coverage:\n\n1. **test_merge_asof_indicator()**: Verifies indicator column creation and content\n2. **test_merge_asof_indicator_custom_name()**: Tests custom naming feature\n3. **test_merge_asof_indicator_false()**: Ensures backward compatibility\n4. **test_merge_asof_indicator_validation()**: Tests error handling for invalid values\n\nRun tests with:\n```bash\npytest pandas/tests/reshape/merge/test_merge_asof.py::test_merge_asof_indicator -v\npytest pandas/tests/reshape/merge/test_merge_asof.py::test_merge_asof_indicator_custom_name -v\npytest pandas/tests/reshape/merge/test_merge_asof.py::test_merge_asof_indicator_false -v\npytest pandas/tests/reshape/merge/test_merge_asof.py::test_merge_asof_indicator_validation -v\n```\n\n## Backward Compatibility\n\n✅ The implementation is fully backward compatible:\n- `indicator` parameter defaults to `False`\n- Existing code without the parameter works unchanged\n- No breaking changes to function signatures\n- No changes to default behavior\n\n## Error Handling\n\nThe implementation validates the indicator parameter:\n- Only accepts boolean or string values\n- Raises `ValueError` with message: \"indicator option can only accept boolean or string arguments\" for invalid values\n- Checks for conflicting column names (_left_indicator, _right_indicator, or custom indicator name)\n\n## Design Notes\n\n### Why Override get_result() in _AsOfMerge?\n- `_OrderedMerge.get_result()` doesn't support indicator\n- `_AsOfMerge` has specific join mechanics requiring custom handling\n- Overriding allows proper integration of pre-merge and post-merge indicator processing\n- Maintains consistency with `_MergeOperation.get_result()`'s indicator handling\n\n### Reusing Indicator Methods\n- `_indicator_pre_merge()` - Adds temporary marker columns\n- `_indicator_post_merge()` - Combines markers into final indicator\n- These inherited methods ensure consistency with regular merge functionality\n- Validation logic is the same across all merge types\n\n## Implementation Checklist\n\n✅ Added `indicator` parameter to `merge_asof()` function signature\n✅ Updated docstring with indicator documentation\n✅ Modified `_OrderedMerge.__init__()` to accept and pass indicator\n✅ Modified `_AsOfMerge.__init__()` to accept and pass indicator\n✅ Implemented `_AsOfMerge.get_result()` with indicator support\n✅ Added comprehensive test cases\n✅ Verified backward compatibility\n✅ Ensured error handling consistency with `pd.merge()`\n✅ Compiled and syntax-checked all modified files\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

This is consistent with a\n        left merge.\n```\n\n## 3. _AsOfMerge Constructor Call in merge_asof()\n\n**File:** `/workspace/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py` (Lines 701-719)\n\n```python\n    op = _AsOfMerge(\n        left,\n        right,\n        on=on,\n        left_on=left_on,\n        right_on=right_on,\n        left_index=left_index,\n        right_index=right_index,\n        by=by,\n        left_by=left_by,\n        right_by=right_by,\n        suffixes=suffixes,\n        how=\"asof\",\n        tolerance=tolerance,\n        allow_exact_matches=allow_exact_matches,\n        direction=direction,\n        indicator=indicator,  #
